name: IDFC FIRST Wealth Credit Card

fees.waiverCondition: Annual fee waived on spends of ₹3,00,000 or more in previous year

categoryRates.description: 6 Reward Points per ₹100 spent on all online and offline purchases
Up to 5X rewards on select dining partners

cappedCategories.description: Rewards capped at 25,000 RP per statement cycle

welcomeBenefits.description: 2,500 bonus Reward Points on first transaction
welcomeBenefits.condition: Make any transaction within 30 days of card issuance

additionalBenefits: 6X reward points on all spends - no category restrictions
Zero interest on cash withdrawal from ATMs for up to 48 days
Low forex markup compared to IDFC FIRST Classic
pros: Extremely low annual fee of ₹499 with easy waiver
6X reward points on ALL spends without category restrictions
Decent lounge access for a card at this price point
Zero interest on ATM cash withdrawals (unique feature)
cons: Per-point value of ₹0.25 means effective return is ~1.5%
25,000 RP monthly cap limits high spenders
idealFor: Users seeking a low-fee card with all-round rewards
Those who want rewards on ALL categories without restrictions
Budget-conscious users who still want premium benefits

faqs.question: What does 6X rewards really mean in value?
faqs.answer: You earn 6 Reward Points per ₹100 spent. At ₹0.25 per point, that's ₹1.50 back per ₹100, or an effective 1.5% return on all spends. While not the highest, it applies to ALL categories.

faqs.question: Do reward points expire?
faqs.answer: No. IDFC FIRST Bank reward points never expire as long as your credit card account is active. This is a unique benefit compared to most banks that expire points in 2-3 years.

faqs.question: How does the zero interest ATM withdrawal work?
faqs.answer: You can withdraw cash from ATMs using your IDFC FIRST Wealth card and pay zero interest if you repay within the billing cycle (up to 48 days). Most other cards charge 2.5-3.5% per month on cash advances.

faqs.question: Is the ₹499 fee waivable?
faqs.answer: Yes, the annual fee is waived if your annual spending exceeds ₹3,00,000. Given the card's benefits, this is a very reasonable threshold.
